[music] It felt like another dream come true, didn't it?  Well, my family and I were hoping the list would come out, everyone was getting together, and then it finally did, and my name was on it. So far, the most memorable game I've had was the U-17 Copa do Brasil final against Bahia, which we were winning 2-0. We conceded the equalizer, but we persevered, and I was able to convert the final penalty to give Vasco da Gama the title. [applause] So many things come to mind, right?  At that moment, the chant I'm going to use is based on pressure; if I make us champions... So there, pressure is sometimes important.  I think there will always be pressure in football, but at that moment the team's supporters always supported me , they gave me peace of mind, and the walk to the ball was long, a lot happened, but thank God I managed to score the goal.  Well, for me it represents a lot of things: that the work is going well, that I'm on the right track. Of course, there's still a lot to happen, and for me, it's also because I'm getting closer to my dream, which is to make my professional debut.  My life outside of Vasco is like that of a regular kid; I spend time with my family, play games, play video games, and fly kites.   That 's the life of a boy who's only 16 years old, right?  Regardless of the responsibility I have, that I carry on my shoulders, I think having this moment to distract myself, being there with my family, laughing, playing, that 's my refuge, that's where I can let go more. It means that our dream is very close. We've fulfilled many dreams; we haven't reached our goal yet, but we're very close.  And to say that this dream, thank God, is coming true and I'm making my family proud. [music] Well, Vasco represents a lot to me, love, affection, it's a club of the people that really welcomes you.  So, for me, Vasco is a feeling, it's a passion, it's hard to describe, but being here in Januário, I come to train here every day, for me it's a defining moment in my life. My biggest dream is to make my debut here at São Januário, to win titles, many titles here as well, and to be another player from the club's youth academy who can go out into the world.  It's remarkable to carry the identity of Vasco, isn't it? It's more about being a local boy.  I think I'm learning a lot of good things here that I can take outside, and that I can be a role model for as well.  Well, the message I leave for the Vasco fans is that I will always honor this giant, heavy jersey, I will always give my best on the field, regardless of the situation, and that you can expect the best from me.
